subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF ECF/Art IV, World Bank, WHO/Africa CDC, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, TI) + international press (Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera) and ReliefWeb on disaster exposure.

Fragile post-civil-war democracy: a disputed 2023 election and a failed Nov-2023 coup (ex-President Koroma charged; 2024 treason convictions), now stabilizing - inflation crushed from ~50%+ to single digits and an IMF program on track - but with a weak health system (a 2025 mpox surge), and high disaster/climate exposure. Its own currency, the leone (SLE, redenominated 2022).

Standout features: a dramatic inflation collapse (~55% in 2023 to ~4-8% in 2025-26) under a stable leone and IMF ECF; an unresolved political-legitimacy crisis from the 2023 disputed vote and failed coup; and severe public-health/climate fragility (Africa's largest 2025 mpox outbreak; the 2017 Freetown mudslide legacy).

economic.notes: Stabilizing off a severe 2022-23 crisis - inflation crushed, an IMF program on track - but a low-income, commodity-dependent, own-currency economy with a recent high-inflation history.

fiscal_state.notes: Consolidating under a 38-month IMF ECF (approved Oct-2024; 1st/2nd reviews completed Dec-2025) - deficit narrowed to ~4.3% of GDP in 2025 with a small primary surplus - but high debt and external-financing dependence. (IMF Dec-2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Its own central bank with nominal autonomy, but a weak credibility record (a 2022 redenomination after near-50% inflation), only now anchoring expectations. (IMF; World Bank)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The leone is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Crushed from ~55% (Oct-2023) to ~4.4% (Oct-2025), but rebounding toward ~8-10% in 2026 on fuel/import shocks - improved yet structurally volatile. (IMF; World Bank)

banking_stability.notes: Small, shallow, and dollarized, with high NPLs and sovereign-exposure concentration. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Narrow and commodity-concentrated - diamonds, iron ore, agriculture (Feed Salone) - with a structural current-account deficit. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Multiparty and IMF-engaged but with weak rule of law, pervasive corruption, and a pressured press.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak with pervasive corruption; the 2024 treason trials over the Nov-2023 coup (mass convictions; ex-President Koroma charged then allowed exile) showed politicized, uneven justice. (Freedom House; TI; Al Jazeera)

statistical_integrity.notes: Limited capacity but IMF-engaged and improving through program reviews. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and under-resourced, exposed by the 2022-23 fiscal/spending overruns and limited service delivery. (IMF/World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Improved after 2020 criminal-libel repeal but still pressured - sedition/defamation tools used against journalists covering corruption. (RSF; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: A peaceful post-war record strained by a disputed 2023 vote and a failed 2023 coup, atop deep poverty.

political_stability.notes: A disputed June-2023 election (Bio re-elected ~56%; APC alleged fraud, EU/Carter Center flagged tabulation opacity) and a failed Nov-2023 coup with a mass prison break; a fragile post-conflict equilibrium since stabilized somewhat. (Al Jazeera; The Conversation; Int'l IDEA)

civil_liberties.notes: Broadly free with active opposition and media, but post-coup security pressure and periodic protest crackdowns (e.g. Aug-2022 unrest). (Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systematic abuse of foreigners; generally open to regional migrants and refugees. (UNHCR; observed)

social_cohesion.notes: An SLPP/APC and regional/ethnic (Mende-Temne) cleavage that recurs around elections, atop a 1991-2002 civil-war legacy. (Freedom House; ICG)

demographics.notes: Very young and growing - a dividend undercut by deep poverty, low human capital, and a jobs strain. (UN; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: An unsanctioned, internationally engaged ECOWAS member outside the Sahel war zone, with the coup the main instability marker.

alliance_reliability.notes: A non-aligned but Western/multilateral-engaged ECOWAS member, currently holding a UN Security Council non-permanent seat (2024-25). (observed; UN)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active armed conflict and outside the Sahel jihadist zone, but the failed Nov-2023 coup is the live instability marker. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Unsanctioned and broadly open, with managed FX and a thin reserve buffer the constraint. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Among the weakest in the dataset - a fragile health system, severe flood/landslide exposure, and poor infrastructure.

climate.notes: ND-GAIN bottom-25 - intense-rain flooding/landslides (2017 Freetown), coastal erosion, very low readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Very weak - an Ebola-scarred system overwhelmed by Africa's largest 2025 mpox outbreak (>50% of new African cases mid-2025), among the world's worst maternal/child mortality. (WHO; Africa CDC; Science)

infrastructure.notes: Poor - unreliable power, limited roads, and low water/sanitation coverage. (World Bank; AfDB)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate, with petty/urban crime and a 2022 drug-epidemic ('kush') public-health-and-safety strain. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration against high flood/landslide exposure. (observed)

summary: Sierra Leone is a fragile post-civil-war democracy in a stabilization phase. A disputed June-2023 election (Bio re-elected ~56%, with the opposition and observers flagging tabulation opacity) and a failed November-2023 coup - a mass prison break followed by 2024 treason trials that convicted dozens and saw ex-President Koroma charged - exposed thin institutions and politicized justice. The brighter story is macro: a cost-of-living crisis that pushed inflation past 50% in 2023 has reversed sharply (to ~4-8% in 2025-26) under a stable leone and an on-track IMF ECF, though the economy stays low-income, commodity-dependent (diamonds, iron ore), and own-currency-volatile. Physical fragility is the standout weakness - a weak, Ebola-scarred health system overwhelmed by Africa's largest 2025 mpox outbreak, plus severe flood/landslide exposure (the 2017 Freetown mudslide) - making for a negatively skewed profile with recovery upside but deep structural and disaster tails.
